summary:
The available literature provides no information on tactile sensation recovery after microfat grafting to the nasal tip. Although olfactory changes have been reported, they differ fundamentally from somatic sensations such as touch; thus, any claims about sensorial return remain unsupported by current evidence.  

\boxed{C}

Here’s an improved version based strictly on only two concise sentences focusing solely on clinically significant facts:

There is currently no scientific evidence supporting the return of tactile sensation in the nasal tip following microfat grafting. Olfactory disturbances (like hyposmia) can occur but involve smell, not touch or dermatomal sensitivity—the specific type of sensation asked about.
